AI Capability Built for Higher Education

Your staff move from anxious bystanders to confident, capable practitioners. Hours saved, tracked and reported - not estimated.

Built by people who worked inside universities, not consultants who studied it from the outside.

Book a free call View our services →

No obligation, no slides, no pitch deck. Just a conversation about where you are.

Where higher education is today

A capability gap that students notice.

“

Students see AI skills as essential, but institutional support lags behind. While over two-thirds of students believe AI skills are essential to thrive in today’s world, fewer than half feel their teaching staff are helping them develop those skills.

HEPI / Kortext Student Generative AI Survey, 2026

Services

AI support designed for every part of the institution.

Whether you need to lift staff capability, equip students for an AI-shaped workplace, or give your leadership team a confident strategic position, we have a programme that fits.

Staff capability

Role-specific AI training for professional services, academic and research staff. Designed to embed, not just to demonstrate.

Learn more →
Student programmes

Structured AI literacy that equips students with the skills, judgement and ethics to use AI confidently in their studies and careers.

Learn more →
Leadership advisory

Practical AI consulting for SLT, deans and HoDs translating strategy into action without losing academic confidence.

Learn more →
Talks and away days

Single-engagement sessions for conferences, leadership days or team events. A confident, sector-aware introduction.

Learn more →
See all our services →

Our flagship offering

The AI Partnership

A wrap-around programme that takes your institution from scattered AI experimentation to a coordinated, evidenced approach. Built around your roles, your policies, and your academic calendar.

  • ✓ Free initial consultation
  • ✓ AI literacy and needs analysis
  • ✓ Personalised workshops, in-person or online
  • ✓ On-demand courses for up to 100 staff
  • ✓ Strategic roadmap session with your SLT
  • ✓ Ongoing AI Café drop-in sessions
  • ✓ Follow-up calls with leadership
Discuss the AI Partnership

Your journey

Wherever you are, we meet you there.

AI adoption isn’t a single moment, it’s a path. These are the stages most institutions move through. We support you at every one.

  1. 01

    Recognising the strategic priority

    AI is on every board agenda. You know you need a coordinated response, not a series of individual experiments.

  2. 02

    Understanding your starting point

    Before you build a plan, you need an honest picture of staff capability, current usage and risk exposure across the institution.

  3. 03

    Building the institutional plan

    Translating insight into a board-ready roadmap with measurable outcomes, accountable owners and a realistic timeline.

  4. 04

    Equipping staff with confidence and capability

    Role-specific training that respects the difference between an academic, a finance officer and a student services lead.

  5. 05

    Embedding and sustaining

    AI evolves quickly. Your institution needs structures that keep momentum after the initial push.

Talk to us about your stage →

We’ve worked inside the buildings we’re trying to help.

Our team brings over a decade of direct experience inside UK higher education, and more than four decades combined across learning, educational technology, and learning and development. That context shapes every workshop we design and every conversation we have with your team.

We know about Clearing pressures. NSS reporting cycles. OfS conditions and condition B. Academic integrity policy at programme level. The gap between schools and professional services. The honest reasons why “just send them an email” doesn’t get staff engagement. The reality of academic scepticism when something new feels imposed.

This isn’t theory for us. It’s the world we came from.

Meet the team →

Free resource

AI use cases for higher education staff.

A practical guide showing how staff across academic and professional services are using AI to save hours every week. Useful as a conversation starter for your own teams.

  • Real examples from teaching, research and professional services
  • Time-saved estimates for common tasks
  • Practical prompts your staff can use today
  • Honest notes on what works and what doesn’t in an HE context
Download the free guide

ROI calculator

How much is the status quo costing you?

Inconsistent, unreviewed AI use has a hidden cost. This calculator shows what a structured approach could reclaim for your institution.

34%

of HE staff say their institution’s stance on AI affects their likelihood to stay. That’s a retention question, not a training question.

Jisc Digital Experience Insights, 2024
